phases.goal: Validate a real problem exists before writing any code or spending money

steps.title: Problem discovery interviews
steps.description: Interview 20 target customers about the problem space. Do NOT pitch your solution. Ask: 'Tell me about the last time you struggled with X.' Listen for pain intensity, frequency, and current workarounds. A problem worth solving has all three.
steps.success_metric: 20 interviews completed, clear recurring pain pattern identified

steps.title: Define the one-sentence problem statement
steps.description: Write: '[Target customer] struggle with [problem] because [root cause], which causes [consequence].' This must come from interview data, not assumptions. Share it with 5 interviewees and see if they nod.

steps.title: Identify existing alternatives and gaps
steps.description: Map every existing solution (competitors, workarounds, spreadsheets). Understand why they fall short for your target customer. Your differentiation comes from this gap.
steps.success_metric: Competitive landscape mapped, clear differentiation documented

steps.title: Define MVP scope (single core feature)
steps.description: Write a one-page MVP spec: target customer, problem, one feature that solves it, and success metric. Ruthlessly cut everything else. The MVP exists to test one assumption, not to be a complete product.
steps.success_metric: MVP spec document complete and reviewed

phases.goal: Ship MVP to first paying customers as fast as possible

steps.title: Build minimum lovable product
steps.description: Use no-code tools first (Bubble, Webflow, Airtable, Zapier). Only write custom code if no-code cannot do the job. Ship ugly and fast. Aim for a working product in 4–8 weeks, not 6 months.
steps.success_metric: 5 beta users actively using the product

steps.title: Charge from day one
steps.description: Even $1 validates that someone finds it valuable enough to pay. Free users give you feedback; paying users give you signal. Start with manual billing if needed — do not spend weeks building payment infrastructure.

steps.title: Weekly user calls
steps.description: Call every active user weekly. Ask: 'What's the one thing that would make you cancel?' and 'What's the one thing you'd tell a friend about?' These answers drive your next 4 weeks of product work.
steps.success_metric: Weekly call cadence established, feedback loop running

phases.goal: Reach $5K MRR or a strong user growth signal that justifies continued investment

steps.title: Identify one repeatable acquisition channel
steps.description: Test 3 channels in parallel (content, paid ads, cold outreach, community, partnerships). Measure CAC and conversion rate. Double down on the channel with the best unit economics. Kill the others.
steps.success_metric: One acquisition channel with measurable, repeatable results

steps.title: Improve retention and NPS
steps.description: Churn kills growth. Measure 30/60/90-day retention. Survey churned users. Fix the top 1-2 reasons people leave before adding new features.

steps.title: Evaluate funding vs. bootstrap
steps.description: If growing organically to $5K MRR: consider staying bootstrapped. If you need to move faster or face a land-grab market: build investor relationships now. Attend startup events, join YC Startup School.

phases.goal: Build the team and infrastructure to grow beyond founder capacity

steps.title: Make first key hire
steps.description: Hire for your biggest bottleneck. If you're engineering-constrained: hire engineer. If sales-constrained: hire salesperson. First hire must be someone you'd work for. Culture starts here.
